title: BigBear.ai Announces First Quarter 2026 Results, Increases Backlog Including More Than $60 million in National Security Contracts, and Affirms Revenue Guidance
text: BigBear.ai reported first-quarter 2026 revenue of $34.4 million, relatively flat compared to $34.8 million in Q1 2025. However, the company achieved significant margin expansion, with gross margins jumping to 34.0% from 21.3% year-over-year, primarily driven by higher-margin generative AI products from the Ask Sage acquisition. The company posted a net loss of $56.8 million versus $62.0 million in the prior year quarter. CEO Kevin McAleenan stated: These wins keep us on track to meet our topline revenue target for 2026. Backlog increased 14% to $281.9 million from the previous quarter, bolstered by a sole-source classified award valued at $53 million. The company also announced approximately $75 million in total wins during the quarter. BigBear.ai strengthened its financial position by converting $124.6 million of 2029 Convertible Notes to equity in January 2026, reducing interest expense by $4.8 million quarterly. The company maintains $431.5 million in total available cash and investments. The company affirmed its full-year 2026 revenue guidance of $135 million to $165 million.
